status_detail: Joint venture between H2V Industry and Hy2gen (Germany) to produce e-SAF at Marseille Fos. 390 MW electrolysis capacity. Total investment EUR 1.5B. Targets 75,000 t/yr e-SAF by 2030. Partners include Technip Energies, Hy24, VINCI Airports. Permitting decision expected mid-2025. Construction start early 2026. Administrative authorization process ongoing.

data_management.narrative_summary: H4 Marseille Fos is a 390 MW electrolysis project at the Fos-sur-Mer industrial zone near Marseille, France. Originally developed by H2V Industry as a 1 GW project, it was restructured in 2025 as a joint venture with Hy2gen (Germany) to produce synthetic aviation fuel (e-SAF) for Marseille Provence Airport. The project brings together Technip Energies (engineering), Hy24 (infrastructure investment), and VINCI Airports. Phase 1 targets 75,000 tonnes of e-SAF per year by 2030, reducing CO2 emissions by 240,000 t/yr. The project is supported by France's Choose France 2025 investment summit and represents one of the largest e-fuel projects in Europe.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at Fos industrial port zone. Dedicated renewable PPAs planned.

sources.title: Hy2gen-H2V H4 Marseille Fos — Choose France 2025 summit announcement
sources.author: Hydronews / FuelCellChina
